title: 11 Herbal Creams For Nerve Pain
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a natural and effective way to relieve nerve pain. Nerve pain, also known as neuropathic pain, occurs when the nerves are damaged or inflamed, causing pain, numbness, and tingling sensations. Herbal teas can help alleviate nerve pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves.  Capsicum annuum, also known as cayenne pepper, contains a compound called capsaicin that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This means that consuming cayenne pepper in the form of tea can reduce nerve pain by interrupting the pain signal.  Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. It also has a compound called gingerol, which has pain-relieving properties. Drinking ginger tea can help alleviate nerve pain and improve circulation, which can speed up the healing process.  Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin can help reduce inflammation and pain in the nerves, making it an effective natural remedy for nerve pain. Drinking turmeric tea can help alleviate nerve pain and improve overall well-being.  Using herbal teas to relieve nerve pain can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help you avoid taking prescription medications that can have harsh side effects. Herbal teas are generally safe and natural, making them a great alternative for those who want to manage their nerve pain without relying on medication. Additionally, herbal teas can help improve your overall health and well-being by reducing inflammation and promoting circulation. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can experience relief from nerve pain and improve your quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams contains capsaicin, a bioactive constituent that helps relieve nerve pain. Capsaicin blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This blocks the nerve pain signals and reduces inflammation, providing relief from nerve pain. Capsaicin also has antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in damaged nerves. By reducing pain and inflammation, capsaicin in Capsicum annuum creams can help manage nerve pain and improve quality of life.
remedies.image_alt: Capsicum annuum herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Capsicum annuum, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Blend the Capsicum annuum into a paste using a food processor or blender.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat.
Add the blended Capsicum annuum paste and beeswax to the coconut oil, stir until beeswax dissolves.
Remove from heat, let cool, and pour into small containers to store the Capsicum annuum cream for nerve pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nerve pain. The active constituent shogaol also contributes to this effect by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. The warming properties of Zingiber officinale creams, due to its gingerols, can increase blood flow to the affected area, helping to reduce swelling and ease pain. The bioactive compound 6-gingerol also has a pain-relieving effect by inhibiting the production of pain-sensing chemicals in the nervous system. By reducing inflammation and blocking pain-causing chemicals, Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate nerve pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of ginger oil (Zingiber officinale), and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, add the shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated. Remove from heat.
Add the ginger oil and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a glass jar or tin.
Let the mixture cool and solidify before use. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains active constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce nerve pain by decreasing inflammation and oxidative stress around the affected nerves. Curcumin specifically has a strong affinity for binding to inflammatory enzymes and proteins, which in turn reduces the production of pro-inflammatory molecules that contribute to nerve pain. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissue and exacerbate pain. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Curcuma longa creams can provide relief from nerve pain and promote a healthy environment for nerve recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, 2 tablespoons of ginger powder, 2 tablespoons of cinnamon powder.
Melt coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add turmeric, ginger, and cinnamon powders. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely. This may take a few hours.
Once solid, your Curcuma longa cream is ready to use. Apply as needed to relieve nerve pain. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids as its key bioactive constituents. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce nerve pain and inflammation. Hyperforin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes that contribute to pain, making it a valuable component in managing nerve pain. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of these compounds help protect nerve cells from damage, promoting healing and reducing pain. By reducing inflammation and protecting nerve cells, Hypericum perforatum creams can provide relief from nerve pain.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of Hypericum perforatum oil, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat. Stir until beeswax is fully melted.
Add shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated and melted.
Remove from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Add Hypericum perforatum oil and peppermint essential oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden completely. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid, isovalerenol, and valepotriates as its bioactive constituents. These compounds work together to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate nerve pain. Valerenic acid, in particular, has been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate nerve pain. The valepotriates in Valeriana officinalis creams also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with nerve pain. By interacting with the nervous system and reducing inflammation, Valeriana officinalis creams may provide relief from nerve pain.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis root, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the dried Valeriana officinalis root to the melted mixture and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides as active constituents. These compounds help to reduce nerve pain by acting as a sedative and relaxing agent. The flavonoids, particularly passiflorine and flavonol, have anti-inflammatory properties that decrease inflammation and alleviate pain in the nervous system. The alkaloids, including passiflorine and harmine, have antispasmodic properties that help to relax muscle spasms and reduce pain caused by nerve irritation. By reducing inflammation and muscle spasms, Passiflora incarnata creams can provide relief from nerve pain and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat.
Add the dried Passiflora incarnata flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture cool and solidify, then whip it with a hand mixer until it becomes a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide as its active constituents. These compounds work together to improve blood flow to the nerves, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that protect the nerves from damage and promote healing. The terpenoids, such as ginkgolides A and B, have anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas. By improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to alleviate nerve pain and promote nerve health.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the shea butter and Ginkgo biloba extract to the melted mixture, stirring until well combined.
Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes. Stir in the peppermint essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let cool completely before use. Apply a small amount to affected area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains active constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate. These volatile oils have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce nerve pain. Linalool in particular has been shown to inhibit the activity of inflammatory mediators, which can contribute to nerve pain. The analgesic properties of linalyl acetate help to block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from nerve pain. Regular application of Lavandula angustifolia creams may help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until combined. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Add lavender essential oil and vitamin E oil, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use for nerve pain relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains active constituents like menthol, menthone, and limonene. These compounds have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties that help with nerve pain. Menthol, in particular, blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This action helps reduce the severity of nerve pain and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of menthone and limonene also reduce swelling and inflammation that can contribute to nerve pain.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove from heat and stir in peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers or a mold to shape the cream.
Let the mixture cool and harden before applying it to the affected area to relieve nerve pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, camphor, and bornyl acetate as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce nerve pain and inflammation. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has a potent antioxidant effect that neutralizes free radicals, which can damage nerve tissue and contribute to pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of camphor and bornyl acetate also help reduce swelling and inflammation around the affected nerve, providing relief from pain. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can help alleviate nerve pain and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1/4 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil and stir well. Remove from heat when the mixture is smooth and melted.
Add the shea butter and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until well combined and the mixture has cooled slightly.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Rosmarinus officinalis cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa creams contains active compounds like THC and CBD, which are absorbed into the skin to provide relief from nerve pain. CBD, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. Another key compound, cannabidiolic acid, has been shown to inhibit the production of pain-promoting molecules in the body. The anandamide-like properties of THC also help increase the production of natural pain-relievers, providing a sense of calm and comfort. When applied topically, these compounds can work directly on the nerve endings to provide targeted relief from pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Cannabis sativa herbal creams for nerve pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons CBD oil, 1 tablespoon vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove melted mixture from heat and stir in CBD oil, vitamin E oil, and peppermint essential oil until well combined.
Pour mixture into a small saucepan and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour cooled mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely before using as a cream for nerve pain.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent nerve pain from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated nerves. These teas may also help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can help prevent nerve pain. Regular consumption may have a positive effect on nerve health.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for nerve pain can be a good option, but consuming them daily might not be suitable for everyone. Some herbs can interact with medications or worsen underlying conditions. It's essential to be aware of the potential risks and effects on your body. Start with small amounts and observe how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with nerve pain, but how long it takes to feel relief varies. Some people notice a difference in a few days, while others take a week or two. The effect may be mild at first, but it can build up over time, helping to reduce discomfort and promote relaxation.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for nerve pain can be most effective in the evening, just before bed. This is because many herbal teas, such as chamomile and lavender, can help calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. A peaceful night's sleep can help reduce nerve pain.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for nerve pain. Some herbs, like St. John's Wort, can affect how your body processes certain medicines. This means you might experience changes in how well the medication works or increased side effects. It's essential to be aware of potential interactions when trying new herbal teas.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas for nerve pain. Some herbs, like pennyroyal and blue cohosh, might not be safe for the baby. If you still want to try herbal teas, stick to those that are commonly used and considered safe, like chamomile or peppermint.
faq_breastfeeding: When consuming herbal teas for nerve pain while breastfeeding, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like chamomile, are generally considered safe, but others, like sage and pennyroyal, may not be. These teas can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. Always check the label and choose teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding.
title_2: 11 Herbal Creams for Stroke Survivors with Nerve Pain
